# S3 Presigned POST (Browser-Based Upload)

**Reference**: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/sigv4-HTTPPOSTConstructPolicy.html  
**Also**: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/HTTPPOSTForms.html  
**Last verified**: 2026-06-15

## Request

```http
POST /{bucket} HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=...
```

The object key is a form field, not part of the URL path.

## Form Fields

### SigV4 authentication fields (generated by SDK)

| Field | Enforced by kumolo | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `key` | yes | Object key; supports `${filename}` substitution; must precede the file part |
| `policy` | no | Base64-encoded policy JSON; accepted and ignored |
| `x-amz-algorithm` | no | `AWS4-HMAC-SHA256`; accepted and ignored |
| `x-amz-credential` | no | `AKID/YYYYMMDD/region/s3/aws4_request`; accepted and ignored |
| `x-amz-date` | no | `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ`; accepted and ignored |
| `x-amz-signature` | no | Hex HMAC signature; accepted and ignored |
| `x-amz-security-token` | no | Accepted and ignored |

### Object metadata fields (optional)

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| `Content-Type` | Stored as object content type |
| `acl` | Canned ACL (e.g. `public-read`) |
| `Cache-Control` | Accepted; ignored by kumolo (no storage path in `ObjectMetadata`) |
| `Content-Disposition` | Accepted; ignored by kumolo (no storage path in `ObjectMetadata`) |
| `Content-Encoding` | Accepted; ignored by kumolo (no storage path in `ObjectMetadata`) |
| `Expires` | Accepted; ignored by kumolo (no storage path in `ObjectMetadata`) |
| `x-amz-meta-*` | Custom user metadata |
| `x-amz-storage-class` | Ignored by kumolo (no-op) |
| `success_action_status` | `200`, `201`, or `204`; controls response code |
| `success_action_redirect` | URL; triggers `303` redirect on success |

### File field

Any part whose field name is not in the known text-field list (see `isPresignedTextField`) is treated as the file part. `key` must appear before the file part (kumolo enforces this; 400 otherwise). Processing stops after the first file part; any fields after the file are ignored.

## `${filename}` substitution

When `key` contains `${filename}`, it is replaced with the `filename` parameter from the `Content-Disposition` header of the file part. Example: `uploads/${filename}` → `uploads/photo.jpg`.

## Policy document

Base64-encoded JSON. kumolo does **not** verify the signature or evaluate policy conditions (consistent with how presigned PUT is handled — see known-limitations). Expiration is not checked.

```json
{
  "expiration": "2026-12-31T23:59:59Z",
  "conditions": [
    {"bucket": "my-bucket"},
    ["starts-with", "$key", "uploads/"],
    ["content-length-range", 0, 10485760]
  ]
}
```

## Success responses

| Condition | Status | Body |
|---|---|---|
| Default (no `success_action_*`) | `204 No Content` | none |
| `success_action_status=204` | `204 No Content` | none |
| `success_action_status=201` | `201 Created` | XML (see below) |
| `success_action_status=200` | `200 OK` | XML (same as 201) |
| `success_action_redirect=<url>` | `303 See Other` | none; `Location` = `<url>?bucket=&key=&etag=` |

`success_action_redirect` takes precedence over `success_action_status`.

201/200 XML body:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<PostResponse>
  <Location>http://{host}/{bucket}/{key}</Location>
  <Bucket>{bucket}</Bucket>
  <Key>{key}</Key>
  <ETag>"{md5}"</ETag>
</PostResponse>
```

## Error responses (standard S3 XML)

| Code | HTTP | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| `InvalidArgument` | 400 | Missing required field (`key` or file part) |
| `NoSuchBucket` | 404 | Bucket does not exist |
| `EntityTooLarge` | 400 | Body exceeds server limit (not enforced in kumolo) |

## SDK mapping

- Go SDK v2: `s3.PresignClient` does not expose `CreatePresignedPost`; use the `s3-manager` presign helper or construct manually.
- JS SDK v3: `@aws-sdk/s3-presigned-post` → `createPresignedPost()`

## kumolo deviations

- Signature is **not** verified (same as presigned PUT).
- Policy conditions are **not** evaluated (expiration, key prefix, content-length-range, etc.).
- `x-amz-storage-class` is accepted and silently ignored.
- `Cache-Control`, `Content-Disposition`, `Content-Encoding`, `Expires` are accepted and silently ignored; `ObjectMetadata` has no fields for them (same limitation as regular PutObject).
- `success_action_status=200` returns the same XML as 201 (real AWS behavior).
- `<Location>` in PostResponse XML uses path-style `http://{host}/{bucket}/{key}`; real AWS emits virtual-hosted `https://{bucket}.s3.amazonaws.com/{key}`.
- SigV4 form fields (`policy`, `x-amz-algorithm`, `x-amz-credential`, `x-amz-date`, `x-amz-signature`) are accepted and ignored; only `key` and the file part are enforced.
